what_it_does: Declares the constitution for the checks/ module: a file-discovered registry of deterministic, pure-verdict check functions that judge values and route chain units via declared pass/fail behavior.
observations: Self-described as a living constitution with prescriptive register status.
Defines checks/ as the file-discovered CHECK registry under G3·S3a.
Each registry row is a pure verdict function: same value yields same verdict, with no model and no time dependence.
Verdicts route chain units by default with annotation; on_fail:'drop' must record every dropped unit.
Currently lists two rows: dossier_refcheck and prose_clean.
Adding a check requires both a new file and an update to this registry.
standouts: The check JUDGES a value — never resolves/dispatches separation-of-powers rule.
The deterministic, model-free, time-free pure-function definition of a check.
The explicit requirement that dropped units are recorded, never silently discarded.
conventions: Uses front-matter metadata with type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, and status.
Written as a constitution document with prescriptive register semantics.
References other documents via wiki-style links in relates-to.
Defines module scope, loader, law, and enumerated rows in a single markdown file.
concerns: The two listed rows are described as wrappers around external files without showing their interfaces or invariants here.
The phrase generalization of the recurring arc is evocative but not operationally defined inside this file.
No explicit schema for a registry row is provided beyond the prose description.
notes: This file is the prescriptive rulebook for the checks module, listing what checks exist and the governance rules they must obey.
questions: What is the exact file-naming or discovery convention that makes a check file-discovered?
What is the schema or format of a registry row file?
How are G3·S3a and the cascade decls ({op:'check', check:'<id>'}) implemented?
What does the radar's relation filter refer to in this context?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as the constitution and registry for deterministic checks; the file does that by declaring scope, law, and an enumerated row list, matching its purpose.
intention_signals: It is trying to turn recurring model-judgment failures on rule-shaped constraints into explicit, nameable, deterministic checks that can be referenced and audited in a processing chain.
novelty: The explicit constitutional framing of a check registry, with a law that checks only judge and never dispatch, and a requirement that every dropped unit is recorded rather than silently filtered.
contribution: A prescriptive, self-documenting registry constitution that names the module's checks, states their invariants, and records the rule that verdicts route but do not dispatch chain units.
summary_for_embedding: The checks/ module constitution file that defines a deterministic, pure-verdict check registry, its loader, its judgment-only law, and its current rows dossier_refcheck and prose_clean.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a prescriptive, discoverable constitution for deterministic checks that judge values and route chain units without resolving or dispatching them.
